steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence: "Like the rest of the books in the series, Master and Commander has a rather abrupt ending, but the following book, Post Captain, picks up neatly where Master and Commander leaves off." Notice the structure: it names a potential flaw (abrupt ending) and immediately provides a fact that softens it (the next book picks up seamlessly).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It demonstrates that those readers who most enjoy reading Post Captain will be those who have just finished reading Master and Commander': The underlined sentence says nothing about which readers enjoy Post Captain most. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It argues that the Aubrey/Maturin series deserves the critical renown of works like The Forsyte Saga': The comparison to The Forsyte Saga appears in the next sentence, not in the underlined portion. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It offers a detail that helps explain why it is not necessarily the case that a certain characteristic of Master and Commander should be considered a flaw': The underlined sentence identifies the "abrupt ending" as a potential flaw, then provides the detail (Post Captain picks up neatly) that explains why that ending isn't necessarily a problem. ✓
  • Choice D — 'It presents a reason Master and Commander and Post Captain are considered to be two of O'Brian's most engaging novels': The passage never claims these are O'Brian's most engaging novels, and the underlined sentence doesn't make that argument. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
